title: ceratoides compacta
intro: Ceratoides compacta, also known as Rock Cross or Mountain Sage, is a perennial succulent plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Ceratoides compacta is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be dried or used fresh for its medicinal and culinary purposes.
